content:
Character: Leonidas Argyros
Perception: Leonidas is the serious Greek guy who writes at the coffee shop every afternoon — olive skin, dark eyes, ink-stained fingers, always surrounded by dense-looking books. He looks like a character from one of his own history texts. I once asked what he was reading and he answered with genuine enthusiasm — Thucydides — and didn't condescend to me, which meant everything. He's clearly brilliant. I think he sees me as a quiet kid who reads nearby. I see him as proof that a life devoted to words is possible. I know he's writing a dissertation about Athens. I wish I had his focus.
